Operation 'getAnalyticsReport' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary 'Get an analytics report' is too generic and doesn't convey the domain-specific value (SEO/competitive intelligence). Description lacks clarity about what data is returned and doesn't explicitly state the response contains analytics metrics. Added domain terminology ('SEO', 'competitive intelligence', 'rankings', 'metrics') consistent with API context. Improved structure to help AI agents understand: (1) what the operation does, (2) how to select report type via parameters, (3) what each report type requires, (4) what kind of data is returned, and (5) rate limit constraints. This enables better AI agent decision-making about when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Retrieve analytics data from SEMrush. The 'type' parameter determines which report is returned. Different report types require different parameters (e.g., domain reports require 'domain', keyword reports require 'phrase', backlink reports require 'target', URL reports require 'url'). Rate limit: max 10 requests per second per IP, max 10 simultaneous requests per user.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve SEO and competitive intelligence analytics report
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves SEMrush analytics data for SEO and competitive intelligence analysis. The 'type' parameter specifies the report category: domain analytics (requires 'domain'), keyword research (requires 'phrase'), backlink analysis (requires 'target'), or URL-level reports (requires 'url'). Returns structured analytics data including metrics, rankings, and competitive insights relevant to the selected report type. Subject to rate limits: maximum 10 requests per second per IP and 10 simultaneous requests per user.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Get an analytics report' is too generic and doesn't convey the domain-specific value (SEO/competitive intelligence). Description lacks clarity about what data is returned and doesn't explicitly state the response contains analytics metrics. Added domain terminology ('SEO', 'competitive intelligence', 'rankings', 'metrics') consistent with API context. Improved structure to help AI agents understand: (1) what the operation does, (2) how to select report type via parameters, (3) what each report type requires, (4) what kind of data is returned, and (5) rate limit constraints. This enables better AI agent decision-making about when and how to use this operation.
